description: CLI for Monarch Money personal finance

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Use to set up Monarch Money credentials. Use --profile/-p to save to a specific profile. Use --force/-F to re-authenticate. Use --credential-type/-c to authenticate a specific type.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use to clear stored credentials. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use to verify you are authenticated. Use --profile/-p to check a specific profile. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Use to refresh an expired token without re-authenticating.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use to verify credentials work against the API. Use --verbose/-v for detailed checks.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to browse profiles. Use --filter/-f to find specific profiles (e.g., is_default:eq:True). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Use to view a profile by NAME. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new profile. NAME is positional and required.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use to switch the default profile used by all commands.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use to permanently delete a profile. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

profiles.usage_instructions: Profile management -- list, get, create, set-default, and delete configuration profiles for multiple environments.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management -- login, logout, check status, refresh tokens, and test credentials. Supports multiple profiles via --profile/-p.

list.help: List all accounts. Filter Examples: monarch accounts list --filter "type:Checking" monarch accounts list --filter "balance:gte:1000" monarch accounts list --filter "institution:like:%Chase%" Other Examples: monarch accounts list monarch accounts list --table monarch accounts list --hidden
list.usage_instructions: Use to browse financial accounts. Use --hidden to include hidden accounts. Filter with --filter/-f (e.g., type:Checking, balance:gte:1000). Default limit is 100. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific account.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get full details of an account by ACCOUNT_ID. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

history.help: Get balance history for an account.
history.usage_instructions: Use to view balance history over time for a specific account. Default returns 30 entries; change with --limit/-l. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

holdings.help: Get securities holdings for a brokerage account.
holdings.usage_instructions: Use to view investment holdings for a brokerage account by ACCOUNT_ID. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: account_ids
arguments.help: [ACCOUNT_IDS]...  Account IDs to sync (all if empty)

sync.usage_instructions: Use to refresh account data from financial institutions. Optionally provide specific ACCOUNT_IDS; syncs all if empty. Use --wait/-w to wait for sync completion.

accounts.usage_instructions: Account management -- list, get details, view balance history, view holdings, and trigger sync. Use --filter/-f with field:op:value (e.g., type:Checking, balance:gte:1000, institution:like:%Chase%).

list.help: List transactions with filtering. Review-Status Filter: monarch transactions list --needs-review            # only "Needs Review" monarch transactions list --reviewed                # only already-reviewed (--needs-review and --reviewed are mutually exclusive.) Filter Examples: monarch transactions list --filter "amount:gte:100" monarch transactions list --filter "merchant:like:%Amazon%" monarch transactions list --filter "category:Groceries" monarch transactions list --filter "is_pending:true" monarch transactions list --filter "amount:gte:50,category:Dining" Supported operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, nin, like, ilike, null, notnull Other Examples: monarch transactions list monarch transactions list --days 30 monarch transactions list --start 2024-01-01 --end 2024-01-31 monarch transactions list --account ACC_ID --table

options.name: --days
options.help: Last N days (shortcut for date range)

options.name: --needs-review
options.help: Only transactions where needsReview == true (Monarch 'Needs Review' filter)

options.name: --reviewed
options.help: Only transactions where needsReview == false (already reviewed)

list.usage_instructions: Use to browse transactions. Filter by date range with --start/-s and --end/-e (YYYY-MM-DD) or --days/-d for last N days. Filter by --account/-a, --category/-c, or --search/-q. Filter by review status with --needs-review (only needsReview==true) or --reviewed (only needsReview==false); these two flags are mutually exclusive and applied server-side. Output rows include needs_review and reviewed_at fields. Use --filter/-f for advanced filtering (amount:gte:100, merchant:like:%Amazon%). Default limit is 500. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific transaction.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get full details of a transaction by TRANSACTION_ID. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

examples: monarch transactions update TXN_ID --notes ""  # Clear notes
update.usage_instructions: Use to modify a transaction's merchant, category, amount, date, notes, or goal. TRANSACTION_ID is positional. Use --notes '' to clear notes. Use --hide-from-reports to exclude from reports. Use --needs-review/--no-needs-review to toggle review status.

recurring.usage_instructions: Use to view recurring transaction patterns. Optionally filter by date range with --start/-s and --end/-e. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

transactions.usage_instructions: Transaction management -- list, get, update, and view recurring transactions. Rich filtering via --filter/-f (amount:gte:100, merchant:like:%Amazon%, category:Groceries). Date ranges via --start/-s, --end/-e, or --days/-d.

list.help: List budgets. Filter Examples: monarch budgets list --filter "category:Groceries" monarch budgets list --filter "budgeted:gte:100" monarch budgets list --filter "remaining:lt:0" Other Examples: monarch budgets list monarch budgets list --month 2024-01 monarch budgets list --table
list.usage_instructions: Use to browse budgets. Filter with --filter/-f (category:Groceries, budgeted:gte:100, remaining:lt:0). View specific month with --month/-m (YYYY-MM) or date range with --start/-s and --end/-e. Default limit is 100. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific budget item.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get details for a specific budget by BUDGET_ID. Optionally specify --month/-m.

budgets.usage_instructions: Budget viewing -- list and get budget details. Filter by category, amount, or remaining. Use --month/-m for specific month (YYYY-MM).

list.help: List all transaction categories. Filter Examples: monarch categories list --filter "group:Income" monarch categories list --filter "name:like:%food%"
list.usage_instructions: Use to browse categories. Filter with --filter/-f (group:Income, name:like:%food%). Default limit is 100. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific category.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get category details by CATEGORY_ID.

categories.usage_instructions: Category management -- list and get transaction categories. Filter by group or name.

list.usage_instructions: Use to browse category groups. Filter with --filter/-f (type:expense, name:ilike:%income%). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific category group.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get category group details by GROUP_ID.

category-groups.usage_instructions: Category group management -- list and get category groups (top-level groupings like Income, Expenses).

list.help: List all transaction tags. Filter Examples: monarch tags list --filter "name:like:%work%" monarch tags list --filter "color:blue"
list.usage_instructions: Use to browse tags. Filter with --filter/-f (name:like:%work%). Default limit is 100. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific tag.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get tag details by TAG_ID.

tags.usage_instructions: Tag management -- list and get transaction tags.

summary.usage_instructions: Use to get a high-level cashflow overview showing income, expenses, and savings. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.help: Get detailed cashflow by period. Filter Examples: monarch cashflow list --filter "group:Income" monarch cashflow list --filter "sum:gte:1000"
list.usage_instructions: Use to view detailed cashflow breakdown. Filter with --filter/-f (group:Income, sum:gte:1000). Use --start/-s and --end/-e for date ranges. Default limit is 100. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get cashflow details for a specific category group.
get.usage_instructions: Use to view cashflow for a specific category group by GROUP_ID. Use --start/-s and --end/-e for date ranges. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

cashflow.usage_instructions: Cashflow analysis -- view summary (income, expenses, savings), detailed breakdowns by period, and cashflow by category group.

list.help: List all linked financial institutions. Filter Examples: monarch institutions list --filter "name:like:%Chase%" monarch institutions list --filter "update_required:true"
list.usage_instructions: Use to browse linked institutions. Filter with --filter/-f (name:like:%Chase%, update_required:true). Default limit is 100. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific linked institution.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get institution details by INSTITUTION_ID.

institutions.usage_instructions: Institution management -- list and get details of linked financial institutions. Filter by name or update status.

list.help: List all merchants available for transaction assignment. Merchants are extracted from cashflow data grouped by merchant, querying the maximum date range to capture all merchants. Filter Examples: monarch merchants list --filter "name:ilike:%amazon%" monarch merchants list --filter "sumExpense:lt:-100"
list.usage_instructions: Use to browse merchants. Filter with --filter/-f (name:ilike:%amazon%, sumExpense:lt:-100). Default limit is 10000. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific merchant.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get merchant details by MERCHANT_ID.

merchants.usage_instructions: Merchant management -- list and get merchants. Default limit is 10000 to capture all merchants. Filter by name or expense amount.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `monarch cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `monarch cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List all transaction rules. Filter Examples: monarch rules list --filter "id:eq:RULE_ID"
list.usage_instructions: List all transaction rules. Use --table/-t for tabular view. Filter with --filter/-f 'field:op:value' (e.g., id:eq:RULE_ID). Use --properties/-p to select fields. JSON by default.

get.help: Get details for a specific transaction rule.
get.usage_instructions: Get full details for a single transaction rule by ID. Use --table/-t for field/value table view. JSON by default.

create.help: Create a new transaction rule. A rule needs at least one criterion (--merchant, --amount, --category-id, or --account-id) and at least one action (--set-category, --set-merchant, --add-tag).

options.name: --expense
options.help: --income          When --amount is set, apply only to expenses (default) or income

options.name: --category-id
options.help: Filter criterion: only match transactions currently in this category ID. Repeatable

options.name: --account-id
options.help: Filter criterion: only match transactions on this account ID. Repeatable

options.name: --set-category
options.help: Action: set this category ID on matching transactions

options.name: --set-merchant
options.help: Action: set this merchant ID on matching transactions

options.name: --add-tag
options.help: Action: add this tag ID. Repeatable

options.name: --use-original-statement
options.help: Match merchant criteria against the original bank statement text

options.name: --apply-to-existing
options.help: Apply the new rule retroactively to existing transactions

examples: monarch rules create --merchant equals:"Whole Foods" --add-tag TAG_ID
create.usage_instructions: Create a new transaction rule. REQUIRES at least one criterion (--merchant 'op:value', --amount 'op:value', --category-id, or --account-id) AND at least one action (--set-category, --set-merchant, or --add-tag). Merchant criterion forms: 'contains:amazon', 'equals:Whole Foods'. Amount forms: 'eq:115.32', 'gt:20', 'between:50:200'. Use --expense/--income to scope amount criteria. Use --apply-to-existing to retroactively apply the rule.

update.help: Update an existing transaction rule. Only the fields you pass are sent.

options.name: --expense
options.help: --income                             When --amount is set, apply only to expenses or income

options.name: --set-category
options.help: Set the setCategory action. Pass empty string to clear

options.name: --set-merchant
options.help: Set the setMerchant action. Pass empty string to clear

options.name: --add-tag
options.help: Replace addTags action with these tag IDs. Repeatable

update.usage_instructions: Update an existing transaction rule. Pass only the fields you want to change. Re-pass --merchant, --amount, --category-id, --account-id to replace criteria. Re-pass --set-category, --set-merchant, --add-tag to replace actions. Pass empty string to --set-category/--set-merchant to clear that action.

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a transaction rule by ID. Use --force/-F to skip the confirmation prompt. Note: the underlying API may report deleted=false even on success; the CLI verifies by re-listing and confirms removal before returning.

rules.usage_instructions: Manage Monarch Money transaction rules (CRUD). Use 'list' to view all rules, 'get <id>' for one rule, 'create' to add a new rule with criteria + actions, 'update <id>' to modify an existing rule, 'delete <id>' to remove one.

usage_instructions: Use for managing personal finances via Monarch Money. Commands follow `monarch <group> <action>` pattern. Most list commands support --table/-t, --filter/-f (field:op:value syntax), --limit/-l, and --properties/-p. Covers accounts, transactions, budgets, categories, cashflow, tags, institutions, and merchants.
